cot_prefix: Okay, so I need to figure out what the main idea of this passage is. Let me read through it again carefully. The passage starts by talking about Alexander Graham Bell inventing the telephone in 1876 and how it revolutionized communication. Then it shifts to discussing how we use phones now—taking photos, accessing the internet, etc.—instead of just talking. Then it moves into the history of mobile phones, starting with the first mobile call in 1973 by Dr. Martin Cooper, how they became available to the public, the cultural aspect with the "yuppie" and how people initially didn't want them. Then the mid-90s brought cheaper phones and SMS, which changed people's timekeeping and writing habits. The passage ends by mentioning how Bell would be amazed at the progress. So the main topic seems to be the evolution of the telephone, specifically into mobile phones, their development over time, and how their use has changed. The answer options are A to D. Let me check each option: A) Alexander Graham's invention. Well, the passage does mention Bell's invention, but it's just the starting point. The main focus isn't just on his invention but on how mobile phones developed from there. B) SMS as a new way of communication. SMS is discussed, particularly towards the end, but the passage is broader than just SMS. It's more about the overall development of mobile phones. C) New functions of the mobile telephone. The passage does mention that phones now have functions like taking photos, etc., but again, the main idea isn't just the new functions but the history and development of the mobile phone over time.
